intro: Iberis visianii, also known as the Italian candytuft or Visian's candytuft, is a perennial plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) native to the Italian Alps. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Iberis visianii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Iberis visianii is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine, and it is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion to alleviate respiratory issues and other health problems.
